intro: Muhlenbergia frondosa, also known as Deergrass or Deer Grass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its delicate, feathery foliage and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Muhlenbergia frondosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal issues, as well as to promote wound healing and skin health.
